title: LESSON: Widget Reversion & Staging Integration Lessons
content:
We completed a comprehensive integration sprint testing a feature-rich dual-view system on the landing page widget, followed by a selective reversion to preserve our minimalist homepage while saving valuable monorepo upgrades across the platform.

1. What We Tried (Staging Widget Curation)

  • Dual-View Architecture: In _widget_staging, we built a secondary layout triggered by clicking an 'Of-The-Day' link below the second Wiktionary word. This loaded an inspiration hub view with multiple feed streams.
  • 8 Integrated Daily Feeds: We integrated live API streams for Wikipedia TFA, PoetryDB, DailyArt Magazine, Chicago Art Institute, Cleveland Museum of Art, Rijksmuseum, a hex Color-of-the-Day generator, and a Font-of-the-Day suggestion card.
  • Wikidata SPARQL Museum Client: We query Wikidata in real-time to retrieve works from major world collections (MOMA, Louvre, MET, SFMOMA, LACMA, Tate) mapped by Wikidata entity IDs.
  • DB-linked Font Settings: We created a controls panel that loaded and stored font style weight/category settings directly into the MySQL database to configure random typeface generation.
  • Anniversary Highlight: A celebratory widget decorated with custom HSL-based gold gradients, golden neon box-shadows, and slow-spinning keyframe micro-animations for anniversary dates.

2. The Windows UNC Path Staging Workaround

  • The Issue: Windows command line environment (CMD) fails to launch node-based applications or build servers when run directly from a UNC network share (\\jeffershizzle-s\Dotcoms\jeffersonwm).
  • The Solution: We created a custom PowerShell pipeline (sync_and_run.ps1) that automatically mirrored the project files to a local SSD cache (C:\Users\Bill\.gemini\antigravity\scratch\widget_staging), ran npm install, started the development server locally on port 3000, and allowed local execution without UNC path restrictions.

3. Selective Reversion and Core Platform Upgrades

  • Discarding Staging UI: To keep the home screen clean and minimalist, we reverted all staging code (server.ts, App.tsx, and index.css) inside _widget_staging back to their original states and completely cleaned up the directory.
  • Retained Monorepo Upgrades: We selectively kept all other improvements made across other services:
    • Feed: A visual calendar-week separator that groups feed logs into structured weekly streams, along with CSS visual improvements.
    • Battalion: The new life RPG emotion database configuration, python script hooks, and updated action-color rule mappings.
    • Lionship: Enhanced package setup and database connection endpoints with tag-based navigation metadata.
